info.name: Madad FastMCP Tools Catalogue
info.description: Official FastMCP Postman collection for the Madad MCP cluster. All tools call Madad backend services through POST /mcp using JSON-RPC. Include Accept: application/json, text/event-stream. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

variable.description: Deployed MCP cluster (Cloud Run). Used by all MCP tool requests.
Workflow agent (VM). Used by the Demo campaign trigger.
RECIPIENT — the SME WhatsApp number the onboarding starts on. Editable.
ONE-TIME: set this variable's CURRENT value (not initial) to SECURITY__JWT_SECRET. Stays local to your Postman, never committed. After that the Demo trigger is one-press.

Madad WhatsApp SENDER (Meta Business number). NOTE: the live sender is set in the backend Meta integration (phone_number_id); this documents which sender is active. Leave blank to use the backend's configured sender.
ONE-TIME: set Current value to SECURITY__WEBHOOK_SECRET (agent HMAC). Never committed.

request.description: Creates a scoped backend channel session for a verified WhatsApp/email identity. Use returned accessToken for existing users and onboardingToken for new leads.
Lists backend event names supported for agent webhook handoff.

Test-emits a configured backend-to-agent webhook. Argument types: event:string required; channel:string|null optional, use email/whatsapp/EMAIL/WHATSAPP; identity:string|null optional email or phone for the agent session; user_id:string|null optional; correlation_id:string|null optional; payload:object|null optional. If backend MCP_AGENT_WEBHOOK_URL is not configured, response is skipped=true.
Check Cloud Run/FastMCP server health. This is the only normal REST endpoint in this collection.
Initialize an MCP session. Stateless FastMCP also allows tools/list and tools/call directly.
List all tools exposed by the Madad FastMCP server.
Small smoke test tool call. Confirms MCP execution and Madad UAT connectivity.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_send_otp. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_verify_otp. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_check_contact. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_onboarding_send_email. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_verify_onboarding_email. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_onboarding_send_phone. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_onboarding_verify_phone. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_complete_onboarding. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_complete_google_onboarding. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_refresh. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_logout. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_auth_google_oauth_url. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_external_send_sms_otp. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_external_send_email_otp. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_external_verify_otp. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

Call FastMCP tool madad_external_send_whatsapp_text. Argument types: to:string required, recipient phone in E.164/digits format without spaces; body:string required; preview_url:boolean optional default false. MCP calls the Madad backend only; the backend owns Meta credentials. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

Call FastMCP tool madad_external_send_whatsapp_template. Argument types: to:string required, recipient phone in E.164/digits format without spaces; template_name:string required approved Meta template name; language_code:string optional, e.g. en_US; components:array<object>|null optional, use null for templates without variables. MCP calls the Madad backend only; the backend owns Meta credentials. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_update_eligibility. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_upload_document. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_upload_document_base64. Argument types: file_name:string required; mime_type:string|null optional such as application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png, application/zip; base64:string required raw base64 only, no data URL prefix; metadata:object required. Metadata requires access_token:string, document_entity_type:string, document_type:string. For CR use document_entity_type BUSINESS_DETAILS and document_type COMMERCIAL_REGISTRATION. For audited financials use document_entity_type BUSINESS_DETAILS and document_type AUDITED_FINANCIAL_REPORT. Optional metadata: kyc_stage:string|null, document_param:string|null, document_label:string|null, from_admin:boolean, target_user_id:string|null. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_upload_commercial_registration. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_upload_audited_financial_report. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_inspect_zip. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_upload_zip_documents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_documents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_business_documents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_financial_documents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_admin_requested_documents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_business_details. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_delete_document. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_update_business_details. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_update_primary_details. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_update_sector_detail. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_complete_stage. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_add_shareholders. Per shareholder, backend-required fields are name:string and phoneNumber:string. Optional fields: firstName:string|null, lastName:string|null, middleName:string|null, email:string|null, address:object|null with zone/streetNo/buildingNo/floorNo/unitNo. The backend derives user/business from access_token; do not send businessDetailsId. Ownership percentage, nationality, documentType, and documentNumber are not accepted by this endpoint. Upload shareholder docs separately after creation. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_shareholders. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_shareholders_kyc_status. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_shareholders_consents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_update_shareholder. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_delete_shareholder. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_upload_shareholder_documents. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_add_buyer. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_buyers. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_get_primary_bank_detail. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_edit_buyer. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_kyc_delete_buyer. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
List active payable products, such as the onboarding and assessment fee product. Requires ADMIN/OPS/SUPER_ADMIN access token.
Create a recorded monetization payment and TESS checkout link. Use this for the QAR 6000 payment gate. Requires user access token via MCP and an idempotency_key.

Send an existing monetization payment link through backend email/SMS delivery. Argument types: access_token:string required; payment_id:string required from madad_payments_create_monetization_payment; recipient_email:string|null optional; recipient_phone:string|null optional; message_title:string|null optional; message_body:string|null optional; idempotency_key:string required. Use null, not empty string, for any unused recipient channel. Reuse the same idempotency_key only when retrying the same send action.
Read a monetization payment record by id. Requires user access token via MCP.
Ask backend to refresh payment status from TESS. Requires user access token via MCP.
Extraction-only invoice upload from WhatsApp/email bytes. Mirrors MSME portal first upload step.
Extract invoice data and immediately submit using extracted fields. This is the default WhatsApp/email agent flow when extracted data is assumed correct.
Submit final invoice upload from bytes when extracted/confirmed fields are already available.
Local file extraction-only invoice upload for development/testing.
Local file extract-and-submit invoice flow for development/testing.
List uploadable invoice files in a ZIP before recursive upload.
Extract a ZIP and recursively upload each invoice through the same backend invoice upload flow.
Get invoices uploaded by the current MSME user.
Call FastMCP tool madad_offers_get_my_offers. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_offers_get_offer. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_offers_get_offers. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.
Call FastMCP tool madad_offers_get_extension_request. Responses are Server-Sent Events; inspect the data line for the JSON-RPC result.

item.name: Demo — Trigger Onboarding Campaign (WhatsApp)
item.description: Kicks off the Madad onboarding agent (Step 0 campaign intro) on WhatsApp for {{demo_phone}}, and starts the run so YES / questions / uploads are handled. Edit the `demo_phone` collection variable to target a different number; everything else is preset. ONE-TIME: set `agent_jwt_secret` (collection variable) to the staging SECURITY__JWT_SECRET — it is intentionally left blank so no secret is committed.

exec: // Self-signs a short-lived HS256 service JWT for the Madad workflow agent.
// REQUIRED ONE-TIME SETUP: set the collection variable `agent_jwt_secret`
// to the staging SECURITY__JWT_SECRET (do NOT commit it).
if (!secret) { throw new Error('Set collection variable agent_jwt_secret to SECURITY__JWT_SECRET first.'); }
var payload = { sub: 'demo-campaign-trigger', iat: now, exp: now + 3600 };

item.name: Failsafe — Trigger Pre-Qualification (webhook)
item.description: Manually fires the prequalification.completed webhook to the workflow agent for {{demo_phone}} (HMAC-signed). Use if the backend's auto-emit on admin pre-qualify doesn't deliver. The user must already be parked after the docs/coffee step. ONE-TIME: set webhook_secret = SECURITY__WEBHOOK_SECRET.

exec: // Failsafe: manually fire the prequalification webhook to the agent so a user
// parked after the docs/coffee step gets their pre-qualification (score + payment)
// message — used if the backend's auto-emit on admin pre-qualify doesn't fire.
// Pick the phone RIGHT IN THE BODY TAB below: replace {{demo_phone}} with a
// literal number, or just edit the `demo_phone` collection variable. The
// signature is computed over the body EXACTLY as sent, so it always matches.
if (!secret) { throw new Error('Set collection variable webhook_secret to SECURITY__WEBHOOK_SECRET first.'); }
// Fresh event id each fire so the agent does not dedupe a repeat trigger.
// Resolve the Body tab exactly as Postman will send it (vars substituted),
